message:
fix(theme): add mutex-based locking to prevent race conditions in parallel tests

The global CURRENT_THEME atomic was susceptible to race conditions when
multiple tests ran in parallel - one test could modify the theme while
another was reading it, causing non-deterministic rendering output.

This was the root cause of the flaky `prop_render_deterministic` proptest
in layout_lab.rs, which would intermittently fail when WCAG contrast tests
modified the theme concurrently.

Changes:
- Add THEME_TEST_LOCK mutex to serialize all theme access
- Add THEME_LOCK_HELD thread-local to detect when current thread holds lock
- Modify set_theme() to acquire lock unless called from within ScopedThemeLock
- Add ScopedThemeLock RAII guard for exclusive theme access during tests
- Add set_theme_internal() for direct theme changes when lock is held

The thread-local flag enables a reentrant-style pattern: when set_theme()
is called from code that's already holding the lock (via ScopedThemeLock),
it sets the theme directly without trying to re-acquire the lock, which
would deadlock.

This fix ensures deterministic rendering in parallel test execution while
maintaining the simple atomic-based fast path for non-test code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
